Name: CurvatureAwarePrecisionRouting
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Mixed-Precision Training
Short Hypothesis: By dynamically adjusting the precision of model parameters based on the curvature of the loss landscape, we can achieve more efficient and stable mixed-precision training, leading to better model performance and reduced computational overhead.
Related Work: While existing works like SNIP [1] and EMQ [2] focus on adaptive mixed-precision training and proxy-based search, respectively, they do not explicitly consider the curvature of the loss landscape. Tri-Accel [7] and DynaExq [6] incorporate some curvature-aware elements, but they are limited to specific aspects and do not provide a comprehensive framework for precision routing. Our proposal aims to fill this gap by integrating curvature diagnostics with precision allocation, ensuring a more principled and efficient approach to mixed-precision training.
Abstract: Mixed-precision training has become a popular technique for reducing computational overhead while maintaining model performance. However, existing methods often rely on heuristic-based precision allocation or static bit-width configurations, leading to suboptimal convergence and instability. In this work, we propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a novel framework that dynamically adjusts the precision of model parameters based on the curvature of the loss landscape. CAPR leverages curvature diagnostics to identify critical regions where higher precision is necessary, ensuring stable and efficient training. We evaluate CAPR on a range of models and datasets, demonstrating significant improvements in both training efficiency and model accuracy compared to state-of-the-art methods.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Curvature Analysis and Precision Allocation

We will first conduct a detailed analysis of the loss landscape to identify regions of high curvature. Using this information, we will design a precision allocation strategy that dynamically adjusts the bit-width of model parameters based on their curvature. We will use the Hessian matrix to quantify curvature and employ techniques such as eigenvalue decomposition to identify critical regions.

We will compare the training efficiency and stability of CAPR against existing mixed-precision training methods on a variety of models, including ResNet-18, EfficientNet-B0, and BERT. We will measure the number of FLOPs, memory usage, and training time, as well as the stability of the training process. We will also evaluate the final model performance on standard benchmarks such as CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and GLUE.

To assess the generalization and robustness of models trained with CAPR, we will perform extensive ablation studies and evaluate the models on out-of-distribution (OOD) datasets. We will also analyze the impact of precision allocation on model robustness to adversarial attacks and noisy inputs.

We will evaluate the scalability of CAPR on larger models and datasets, such as Vision Transformers (ViTs) and large language models (LLMs). We will also explore the deployment of CAPR on edge devices, focusing on memory efficiency and inference speed. We will compare the performance of CAPR with other state-of-the-art mixed-precision training methods in these settings.
Risk Factors and Limitations: 1. **Curvature Estimation Accuracy**: The accuracy of curvature estimation can significantly impact the effectiveness of precision allocation. We will need to develop robust and efficient methods for curvature diagnostics.

2. **Computational Overhead**: The additional computational overhead of curvature diagnostics and dynamic precision allocation must be carefully managed to ensure that the overall training process remains efficient.

3. **Model and Dataset Dependence**: The performance of CAPR may vary across different models and datasets. We will need to conduct extensive experiments to validate its effectiveness in diverse settings.

4. **Stability and Convergence**: Ensuring stable and consistent convergence with dynamic precision allocation can be challenging. We will need to carefully monitor and adjust the precision allocation strategy during training.
